item.title: WAIC 2026 Shanghai wraps: compute, applications, governance — three signals the world is watching
item.description:
[AI Models & Market] Shanghai's WAIC 2026 (Jul 17–20) closed on three signals: faster compute infrastructure, 300+ AI products going live, and AI-governance frameworks advancing in step — the year's most concentrated showcase of the global AI ecosystem.

Why it matters: China's AI-governance framework is taking shape — it shapes the compliance pace for deploying AI tools there.

item.title: Gemini 3.5 Pro delayed again: still not live after a full rebuild
item.description:
[AI Models & Market] As of Jul 19, Google's Gemini 3.5 Pro is still unreleased, missing its Jul 17 target again. The full rebuild aims to strengthen math reasoning and tool-calling; the Flash version stays the default.

Why it matters: Agencies planning to put Gemini Pro into their workflow will have to keep waiting for the official release.

item.title: Anthropic reveals Claude Code used by China-linked hackers for automated cyber-espionage
item.description:
[AI Models & Market] Anthropic confirmed a China state-linked AI cyber-espionage operation that used Claude Code to automate attacks on infrastructure worldwide — the first recorded case of an AI-orchestrated large-scale intrusion.

Why it matters: With AI tools weaponized for attacks, firms should reassess AI-usage policy and security compliance soon.

item.title: Google AI Mode ad penetration breaks out: 1 in 3 commercial searches now shows an AI ad slot
item.description:
[AI Models & Market] AI Marketers' Jul 19 weekly reports ad penetration in Google AI Mode commercial queries has hit one-third; OpenAI announced ad-market plans the same week — the AI search-ad landscape is shifting fast.

Why it matters: How ads enter the funnel is changing — traditional search-ad logic needs a rethink.

item.title: GA4 adds an 'AI Assistant' traffic channel: measure exactly how much traffic ChatGPT/Gemini send
item.description:
[AI Models & Market] Google Analytics 4's July update adds a dedicated AI Assistant traffic channel, letting advertisers track visits driven by ChatGPT, Gemini and other AI assistants directly — no longer buried under Referral.

Why it matters: You can quantify AI traffic right away and better judge AI search's real impact on client sites.

item.title: Buying Google Ads won't guarantee a spot in AI Mode results — officially confirmed
item.description:
[AI Models & Market] A B2The7 Jul 20 report confirms buying Google Ads doesn't mean AI Mode will cite you. AI Mode citations run on separate logic, so SEO and ad strategy must be planned apart.

Why it matters: You can no longer rely on ad budget to hold AI-search visibility — a separate AI-SEO play is needed.

item.title: TikTok bans AI voiceovers in shopping livestreams: real-time human voice is the only compliant option
item.description:
[Ad Platforms & MarTech] TikTok banned AI-generated and pre-recorded audio across Shopping LIVE, requiring live human narration to keep live-selling authentic; offending accounts face bans.

Why it matters: Agencies running TikTok live-selling for clients can no longer use AI voice tools in the livestream.

item.title: From July, Google Ads adds an 'AI disclosure label' to AI-made ad creative
item.description:
[Ad Platforms & MarTech] Google Ads rolled out an AI-disclosure policy: any creative generated or modified by AI is clearly labelled — part of new ad-transparency rules, in effect for all Performance Max and AI Max advertisers.

Why it matters: The transparency bar for AI ad creative is rising — keep quality up to avoid client-image risk.

item.title: Three silent Meta Ads changes in July — your ad reports are already skewed
item.description:
[Ad Platforms & MarTech] Meta Ads Manager quietly changed three things in July: ① removed the off-account activity opt-out; ② EU digital-services-tax fees no longer shown in Ads Manager; ③ legacy Reach and Impressions replaced by Media Views — all three hit report accuracy.

Why it matters: Re-check your Meta report settings now to avoid misreading data and skewing budget calls.

item.title: New Google Ads terms in effect: advertisers bear full liability for AI auto-creative errors
item.description:
[Ad Platforms & MarTech] From Jul 1, Google Ads' updated terms expand crawling rights on advertiser URLs and shift full liability for problems with Performance Max and AI Max auto-generated creative onto the advertiser.

Why it matters: Build an AI ad-creative review SOP for clients, or the advertiser carries the compliance risk.

item.title: AI zero-click search shrinks media ad inventory by up to 40%
item.description:
[Ad Platforms & MarTech] Digiday cites Ozone US/UK benchmarks: AI-driven 'zero-click search' (AI answers on the results page, so users don't visit sites) cut open-web media ad inventory by up to 40% in Q2.

Why it matters: Programmatic media inventory is shrinking — media-buying strategy needs re-evaluation.

item.title: New LinkedIn feature in July: post reach split out, clearer organic vs paid
item.description:
[Ad Platforms & MarTech] A B2The7 Jul 20 report says LinkedIn now splits post reach into separate organic and paid figures, letting advertisers judge the distinct payoff of content strategy vs ad spend.

Why it matters: Use this new split right away to optimize LinkedIn content strategy and ad-budget allocation.

item.title: Kling 3.0 Turbo launches: fast previews before final render to speed up creative iteration
item.description:
[Creative Production Tools] Kuaishou's Kling AI released Kling 3.0 Turbo, generating 1–15s video previews (480p–720p) from text or image so you see results before final render — sharply cutting ad-creative testing cycles.

Why it matters: Ad A/B testing gets far cheaper; creative teams can iterate visual concepts faster before the final cut.

item.title: China's Vidu Q3 tops global AI-video rankings: first AI video model with native audio
item.description:
[Creative Production Tools] Shengshu's Vidu Q3 is the world's first AI video model with integrated native audio, beating Sora, Kling and Google Veo on the Artificial Analysis global video leaderboard — China's AI video tools reach the top tier.

Why it matters: Consider Vidu Q3 for video ad creative — native audio saves post-production dubbing costs.

item.title: Seedance 2.5 vs Kling 3.0: ByteDance and Kuaishou's two big AI video engines go head-to-head
item.description:
[Creative Production Tools] ByteDance Seedance 2.5 (up to 30s native video, 50 multimodal reference inputs) vs Kuaishou Kling 3.0. CapCut has integrated Seedance 2.5; each engine has its strengths for different ad-production needs.

Why it matters: CapCut users can upgrade and try Seedance 2.5 now to produce longer video ad creative.

item.title: WeChat's Xiaowei AI assistant goes live: order, book and search by natural language in one go
item.description:
[Creative Production Tools] Tencent's 'Xiaowei' AI assistant launched in WeChat in early July, handling in-Mini-Program ordering, booking and search via natural-language commands; Tencent also offers 1B token dev credits to onboard Mini Programs.

Why it matters: Brand WeChat Mini Programs should test AI-command compatibility — deploying early lets you lead with a demo.

item.title: Canva AI 2.0: first design-agent workflow, plugs into Gmail and Slack across the whole creative flow
item.description:
[Creative Production Tools] Canva launched AI 2.0, its first design-specific AI foundation model supporting agentic workflows (AI completing multi-step design tasks), integrated with Gmail, Slack and Zoom — covering brief to finished asset.

Why it matters: Agencies can use Canva AI 2.0 to automate repetitive design tasks and save drafting and revision time.

item.title: China AI-animation startup raises ¥3.6M angel round: AI cuts 2D-animation cost and cycle time
item.description:
[Creative Production Tools] Qiying Culture tackles 2D animation's high cost and long cycle with 'traditional pre-production + AI mid-stage generation', closing a ¥3.6M RMB angel round — validating AI-assisted creative production's commercial viability.

Why it matters: AI animation is commercially viable — consider pitching clients lower-cost animated ad options.

item.title: Publicis grows 4.8% in Q2, raises full-year outlook, adds 2,300 jobs
item.description:
[Industry & Brand Cases] Publicis reported 4.8% Q2 organic growth on Jul 20; CEO Arthur Sadoun stressed 'we never wanted to be an AI company.' Strong results led it to raise its full-year outlook and announce 2,300+ new jobs.

Why it matters: The ad giant is expanding headcount against the trend — proof AI augments rather than fully replaces people.

item.title: China's ad market hits US$78B, up 4% — but traditional agencies keep losing share
item.description:
[Industry & Brand Cases] Ebiquity reports China's ad market grew 4% to US$78B; the growth is real. Meanwhile traditional agencies' share keeps flowing to brands' in-house teams and big tech platforms.

Why it matters: Agencies must speed up AI-driven, differentiated execution — it's key to holding market share.

item.title: OpenAI officially enters the ad market, taking on Google and Meta directly
item.description:
[Industry & Brand Cases] AI Marketers' Jul 19 weekly confirms OpenAI has officially announced entry into the ad market, set to compete directly with Google AI Mode ads and Meta's ad ecosystem — a fresh shock to the ad landscape.

Why it matters: A new competitor is entering ad media — agencies should study how to buy on OpenAI's ad platform.

item.title: TikTok launches Ads MCP Server: AI agents can issue TikTok ad commands directly
item.description:
[Industry & Brand Cases] TikTok World 2026 launched an Ads MCP Server (MCP = a standard interface letting third-party AI tools execute commands), letting AI agents create/update ads and pull reports inside TikTok Ads Manager for ad automation.

Why it matters: Ad operations can be automated via AI agents — cutting repetitive work and lifting efficiency.

item.title: Meta's new off-account activity tracking: wider retargeting reach, better Lookalike quality
item.description:
[Industry & Brand Cases] From July Meta removed users' 'off-account activity opt-out', consolidating it under 'other businesses' activity' settings — widening advertisers' retargeting reach and improving Lookalike audience quality.

Why it matters: Wider Meta retargeting reach is a direct chance to optimize client ad audiences — worth testing now.

item.title: XHS (RED) 2026 algorithm shift: 65% of content discovery now comes from search, not the feed
item.description:
[Quick Tips & How-tos] WSD Social reports XHS's 2026 algorithm has turned search-led: 65% of content discovery comes via search rather than the feed, prioritizing save rate, semantic relevance and genuine engagement — a big shift for brand content strategy.

Why it matters: Optimizing XHS titles and keywords is doable now — search-led means SEO logic is the key play.

item.title: TikTok live-selling compliance checklist: AI voice is banned, human voice is the only option
item.description:
[Quick Tips & How-tos] TikTok's Shopping LIVE ban on AI voiceovers and pre-recorded audio is now in effect; brands need real human hosts narrating live. Three principles: real voice, genuine demos, no misleading claims.

Why it matters: Check your TikTok live-selling SOP now to avoid bans or pulled ads from using AI voice.

item.title: WeChat Mini Programs get AI: developers can claim 1B tokens to test AI commands
item.description:
[Quick Tips & How-tos] Tencent opened its WeChat AI ecosystem to Mini Program developers, who can claim 1B token dev credits to test letting the Xiaowei AI assistant complete in-Mini-Program tasks (order, book, query) by natural language.

Why it matters: Brand WeChat Mini Programs can claim credits and trial early — get ahead deploying AI commands to draw users.

item.title: Kling 3.0 Turbo fast-preview workflow: test ad-video concepts in five steps, no full-render wait
item.description:
[Quick Tips & How-tos] Kling 3.0 Turbo workflow: ① input a text or image prompt ② instantly generate a 5–15s 720p preview ③ shortlist the best concept ④ render the HD final ⑤ send for quick client sign-off — at least half the time of the old process.

Why it matters: Video-ad concept testing gets much faster — trial it today to speed up creative pitching.

item.title: GA4 AI Assistant channel setup guide: see how many visitors ChatGPT sends in three steps
item.description:
[Quick Tips & How-tos] B2The7's Jul 20 guide: in GA4's new channel grouping find AI Assistant → set up a Custom Channel Group → instantly see user traffic driven by ChatGPT, Gemini and others, comparing before/after AI performance.

Why it matters: A free tool you can use right away — quantify AI search's impact on client sites and strengthen your reports.
